text:
Now let me run the counts. I'll count:

(a) UNRESOLVED Sentry issues (status != 'resolved')
(b) Linear issues NOT in 'done' and NOT in 'canceled' state 
(c) OPEN GitHub pull requests whose titles mention "SSO SCIM provisioning"

Let me run …(+18)

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as unresolved_sentry_issues FROM sentry_issues WHERE status != 'resolved'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as active_linear_issues FROM linear_issues WHERE state != 'done' AND state != 'canceled'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as sso_scim_prs FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'open' AND LOWER(title) LIKE '%sso scim provisioning%'"}

text: Now let me get the grand total by running a single query that combines all three counts
